video:video.video:title: Lights out in Havana’s nightlife after U.S. oil blockade
video:video.video:description: There are few lights on the streets, no families out for a stroll, no couples canoodling in central Havana. (AP video: Ariel Fernández & Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape testament to nature’s resilience
video:video.video:description: Four decades on, Chernobyl remains too dangerous for humans. But the wildlife has moved back in. Wolves now prowl the vast no-man’s-land spanning Ukraine and Belarus, and brown bears have returned after more than a century.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian police shoot and kill a gunman who killed at least 6 and took hostages in a supermarket
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, before taking hostages and barricading himself inside a supermarket on Saturday has been shot and killed by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, April 18th: Iran fully closes Strait of Hormuz over US blockade; Mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves six dead; Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics; Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest.

video:video.video:title: Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest, leaving heavy damage but no deaths
video:video.video:description: A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the U.S. on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris.

video:video.video:title: 500 fans turn Santiago into a Star Wars galaxy
video:video.video:description: Star Wars fans gathered Saturday for the third edition of the “Parade of the Galaxies,” an event featuring a variety of cosplayers who showcased their best costumes along a main avenue in central Santiago.

video:video.video:title: Reenactors hope to keep the memories of fallen American Revolution soldiers alive in Lexington
video:video.video:description: The Lexington Minutemen are marking the anniversary of the Battle of Lexington in Massachusetts on Saturday, as they do every year.

video:video.video:title: Obama and Mamdani sing Wheels on the Bus to preschoolers
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama met with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani for the first time on Saturday and led Bronx preschoolers in a singalong. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Eyewitnesses recount three deadly Israeli strikes on medics in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: “We went to help. These aren't just civilians, they're our colleagues, so it was a little emotional for us,” Mohammed Jaber, 43, told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Pope calls for 'structures of solidarity' to help marginalised as he wraps up Cameroon visit
video:video.video:description: Friday marked the half-way point in Leo’s 11-day tour of four African nations. He leaves Saturday for Angola, another country blessed with oil and other natural resources, but where a third of the population lives on less than $2.15 a day.

video:video.video:title: Gaza's garbage crisis adds another threat to the health of Palestinians
video:video.video:description: Since a fragile ceasefire began last October, conflict in the region has calmed, but much of the territory remains in ruins with no clear timeline for reconstruction.

video:video.video:title: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it
video:video.video:description: The announcement was made the day after U.S. President Donald Trump said the American blockade “will remain in full force” until Tehran reaches a deal with the U.S., including on its nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade
video:video.video:description: Confusion over the critical chokepoint threatened to deepen the energy crisis roiling the global economy and push the two countries toward renewed conflict, even as mediators expressed confidence a new deal was within reach.

video:video.video:title: You can put beef tallow and salmon sperm on your face. But should you?
video:video.video:description: Animal-derived skincare is trending, from salmon sperm facials to moisturizers made with beef tallow.

video:video.video:title: Iran's supreme leader vows more defeats for US and Israel at hands of Iranian navy
video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader vows more defeats for US and Israel at hands of Iranian navy

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV says he doesn't want to debate Trump, but will keep preaching the Gospel
video:video.video:description: Speaking to reporters, Pope Leo XIV addressed head the spiralling back-and-forth saga of Trump’s critiques of his peace message, which have dominated news headlines this week.

video:video.video:title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics, including the controversial drug ibogaine
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, which recently has been embraced by combat veterans and conservative lawmakers despite having serious safety risks.

video:video.video:title: French soldier killed in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon on second day of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The attack near the southern Lebanese village of Ghandouriyeh came after a 10-day ceasefire went into effect at midnight Thursday between Israel and Lebanon's militant Hezbollah group.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran 'got a little cute,' but there are good conversations happening
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says that U.S. talks with Iran are going well and that he expects to have more information “by the end of the day

video:video.video:title: Preparations as Trump says US negotiators will be in Pakistan on Monday for talks with Iran
video:video.video:description: Iran did not immediately confirm the talks but its chief negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammed Bagher Qalibaf, said in an interview aired on state television late Saturday that “there will be no retreat in the field of diplomacy,” while acknowledging a wide gap remained between the sides.

video:video.video:title: Pope leads mass in Angolan city of Kilamba
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived by popemobile before leading Mass on Sunday in the Angolan city of Kilamba.

video:video.video:title: Pope calls for Angolans to took to the future
video:video.video:description: Leo celebrated Mass before an estimated 100,000 people outside the capital and again sought to encourage Angolans.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits Catholic shrine in Angola that was a center of African slave trade
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited Sunday a Catholic shrine in Muxima, Angola, a former epicenter of the African slave trade, as he arrived in the southern African country a day earlier with a message of encouragement for its long-suffering people.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 19
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 19th: Trump says U.S. talks with Iran in Pakistan Monday; Louisiana shooting kills eight children; Robot wins Beijing half-marathon, beating human record; Kite festival kicks off in northern France.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it won’t hand over enriched uranium to the US
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh said his country isn't for a new round of face-to-face talks with the US because the Americans “have not abandoned their maximalist position.”

video:video.video:title: Belgium’s Beguinages: Tranquil oases in a world of noise and distraction
video:video.video:description: Nestled in a sea of yellow daffodils lies an oasis of calm and tranquility founded in 1245: the Princely Beguinage Ten Wijngaerde of Bruges.

video:video.video:title: New rare earth mineral facility using new extraction technique set to open in South Africa
video:video.video:description: A new rare earth mineral facility that uses what it claims to be a ground-breaking extraction technique is set to open in South Africa, with US investment.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drones hit industrial facilities and maternity ward in Russia
video:video.video:description: Russia’s Ministry of Defense said that its forces had destroyed 258 Ukrainian drones overnight on Saturday over 16 Russian regions, as well as over the annexed Ukrainian peninsula of Crimea and the Black and Azov seas.

video:video.video:title: International Kite Festival kicks off in Berck-Sur-Mer on French Atlantic coast
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of participants are expected from all over the world for the week-long event that started on April 18 and ends on April 26.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese army fills riverbeds and opens some roads in south to replace destroyed bridges
video:video.video:description: During Israel’s invasion of southern Lebanon over the past weeks, Israel’s air force destroyed several bridges on the Litani river.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's interior ministry investigating shooting in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in the streets of Kyiv Saturday before taking hostages and barricading himself in a nearby supermarket has been shot dead by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: Leaders vote in Bulgaria's national elections
video:video.video:description: The election could bring to power a left-leaning ex-president just days after Hungarian voters rejected the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement of Viktor Orbán in Hungary.

video:video.video:title: Eddie Murphy arrives to receive AFI Life Achievement Award
video:video.video:description: Kenan Thompson, Keegan-Michael Key and Mike Myers pay tribute to Eddie Murphy's talent and legacy, ahead of a star-filled American Film Institute ceremony. (April 19)

video:video.video:title: Archbishop of Miami speaks on Catholic charity funding cuts to house migrant children
video:video.video:description: Archbishop of Miami Thomas Wenski raises concerns about the Trump administration's decision to cancel an $11 million contract used to shelter migrant children entering the United States.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana father kills 7 of his children plus another child in shooting at house, police say
video:video.video:description: Officials say a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two other people Sunday in an early morning attack of domestic violence carried out across two houses.

video:video.video:title: People in south Lebanon find widespread destruction as they return after fragile ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents of southern Lebanon returned on Sunday to their homes and shops but were shocked by the widespread destruction caused by 6-weeks of war between Hezbollah and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Police describe Louisiana shooting scene where children were found dead as 'incredibly gruesome'
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Shreveport, Louisiana, say a gunman killed eight children in domestic-related shootings at two different homes.

video:video.video:title: US seizes Iranian-flagged ship near Strait of Hormuz that tried to pass blockade
video:video.video:description: The United States said it forcibly seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to pass its naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, the first such interception since the blockade of Iranian ports began last week.

video:video.video:title: UK police investigating if arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies
video:video.video:description: British police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies.

video:video.video:title: US taxpayers help Cyprus upgrade military installations for humanitarian aid
video:video.video:description: Cyprus' main Evangelos Florakis naval base, just 142 miles (229 kilometers) from Lebanon’s coast, will get a new, U.S.

video:video.video:title: Fire in Malaysian Borneo water village destroys about 1,000 homes, displaces thousands
video:video.video:description: A fire that tore through a Malaysian coastal settlement on Borneo Island destroyed about 1,000 homes and displaced over 9,000 people, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robot runs half marathon race in Beijing faster than human world record
video:video.video:description: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the 21-kilometer (13-mile) race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, according to a WeChat post by the Beijing Economic-Technological Development Area, also known as Beijing E-Town, where the race kicked off.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana mass shooting victims mourned at vigil
video:video.video:description: Mourners gathered in a parking lot to honor the eight children killed in a Louisiana mass shooting. (AP Video/Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: BookCon returns after six-year hiatus
video:video.video:description: After a six-year hiatus, BookCon 2026 is back to New York and bringing literature lovers together. (April 19)

video:video.video:title: Seven million tulips bloom at Keukenhof garden in Netherlands
video:video.video:description: Seven million flowers are blooming at a famous garden in the Netherlands. Keukenhof opens for just eight weeks a year to showcase its colourful display of tulips.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in Caribbean Sea
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it launched another strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, killing three people Sunday.

video:video.video:title: A look at life on board an Iraqi commercial oil tanker amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: On board an oil tanker navigating the tense waters between the Gulf of Oman and the Strait of Hormuz, Iraqi Captain Rahman Al-Jubouri continues to work in one of the world’s most volatile maritime corridors, where the U.S.

video:video.video:title: US Navy seizes an Iranian-flagged cargo ship
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. forcibly seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get around a naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, the first such interception since the blockade of Iranian ports began last week.

video:video.video:title: New breed of humanoid robots trained to maintain China's power grid
video:video.video:description: Humanoid robots are being trained to carry out maintenance on China’s power grid. Developers say the machines could eventually handle repetitive and hazardous work.

video:video.video:title: UNESCO-protected embroidery inspires modern craftswomen in Bosnia
video:video.video:description: In the old Bosnian city of Banya Luka a traditional style of embroidery is not just becoming more popular, it's becoming fashionable.
